reasoning: As of late 2025, there are no approved or licensed vaccines for Shigellosis (bacillary dysentery). The most advanced candidate is Valneva's S4V, which received FDA Fast Track designation in late 2024 but is still fundamentally a clinical-stage candidate. Vaccine development and the regulatory approval process (BLA submission and review) typically take multiple years. Since there is no pending late-stage regulatory application that could realistically be approved by February 2026, the scientific and regulatory probability of a new vaccine being created and officially approved in this 1 to 3-month horizon is effectively zero. The only remaining source of uncertainty is the possibility of an erroneous or premature edit to the Wikipedia article ("List of infectious diseases") that goes un-reverted on the specific resolution dates. Given the high profile of medical pages on Wikipedia and strict sourcing requirements, this risk is extremely low. Thus, the probabilities for all three dates remain close to the minimum allowable bound.
